prompt:
Construct a FitCommerce Operations & Inventory Suite supporting offline-first management for a fitness club that runs member-driven group-buy campaigns for gear and supplements while maintaining tight operational control. Roles include Administrator (system configuration and access control), Operations Manager (catalog, inventory, reporting), Procurement Specialist (suppliers and purchase orders), Coach (class readiness and limited reporting), and Member (browse items and join group-buys). The React web interface presents a dashboard KPI hub that summarizes member growth and churn, renewal rate, engagement, class fill rate, and coach productivity with time-based toggles (daily/weekly/monthly) and filters by location, coach, item category, and date range; access to each report is permissioned and exports generate downloadable CSV or PDF files with a timestamped naming convention. Item listing screens support multi-spec attributes such as category, brand, condition (new/open-box/used), refundable deposit (default $50.00, configurable per item), and billing model (one-time or monthly rental), along with available time windows (e.g., 03/27/2026 9:00 AM–5:00 PM) and inventory quantity; staff can save drafts, publish/unpublish, and batch-edit prices or availability with immediate inline validation feedback. Members can start or join a group-buy from an item detail view, see progress toward the minimum quantity, and receive clear on-screen outcomes for success or failure at the cutoff time; orders support notes and a visible operation timeline so staff can explain adjustments, splits, or cancellations without ambiguity.

The backend uses Go with Echo to expose a decoupled REST-style API consumed by the React UI over the local network (or same machine), with PostgreSQL as the system of record for items, inventory snapshots, group-buy campaigns, orders, suppliers, purchase orders, reports, and immutable audit events. Business rules enforce inventory and availability consistency: publishing is blocked if required specs are missing, if availability windows overlap with an item's blackout windows, or if quantity is negative; batch edits run the same validation and fail atomically per row with a reason stored for review. Group-buy and order processing follows a strict status machine (create, pay, cancel, refund) using offline settlement markers (no external payment); unpaid orders auto-close after 30 minutes, campaigns evaluate at cutoff and mark success only if the committed quantity meets the threshold, and fulfillment can split or merge orders by supplier, warehouse bin, or pickup point while preserving a complete operation log. Procurement supports supplier profiles and a closed-loop purchase order flow (create, approve, receive, return, void) with variance handling for shortages/overages and unit price differences; received variances must be resolved within 5 business days via adjustment or return, and procurement cost accounting rolls up landed cost per item and period for reconciliation summaries. Security and privacy requirements include salted password hashing (Argon2id), five consecutive login failures triggering a 15-minute lockout with on-device CAPTCHA, server-side session expiration after 30 minutes idle and 12 hours absolute, strict privilege checks on every endpoint, and comprehensive SQL injection/XSS protections via parameterized queries and output encoding. Biometric data, if stored for facility access, is encrypted at rest using AES-256 with envelope-style key rotation every 90 days and encrypted in transit via TLS even on LAN; sensitive fields are masked in UI and exports based on role, retention defaults to 7 years for financial/procurement records and 2 years for access logs, and deletions are traceable via tamper-evident audit logs. Nightly local backups are written to an admin-configured path with encrypted archives and checksum verification, ensuring the entire system remains fully functional without any internet dependency.
